{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Misaki, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
[[file:080504 Sairyou Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|right|Aerial photo of Sairyō Kofun]]
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Sairyo Kofun}} *[http://www.misakicho-kanko.com/spot/watching/detail_000044.html Misaki Town Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.town.misaki.osaka.jp/soshiki/kyouiku_iinkai/shogai/bunkazai/801.html Misaki town home page] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:History of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Misaki, Osaka]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Misaki, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
[[file:080504 Sairyou Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|right|Aerial photo of Sairyō Kofun]]
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Misaki, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
[[file:080504 Sairyou Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|right|Aerial photo of Sairyō Kofun]]
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category::kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category::-inline|Sairyo Kofun}} *[http://www.misakicho-kanko.com/spot/watching/detail_000044.html Misaki Town Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} *[http://www.town.misaki.osaka.jp/soshiki/kyouiku_iinkai/shogai/bunkazai/801.html Misaki town home page] {{in lang|ja}}

In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Misaki, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
[[file:080504 Sairyou Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|right|Aerial photo of Sairyō Kofun]]
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers

It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Misaki, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
[[file:080504 Sairyou Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|right|Aerial photo of Sairyō Kofun]]
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers

{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>

The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Misaki, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
[[file:080504 Sairyou Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|right|Aerial photo of Sairyō Kofun]]
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers

According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.

These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Misaki, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
[[file:080504 Sairyou Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|right|Aerial photo of Sairyō Kofun]]
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Misaki, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
[[file:080504 Sairyou Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|right|Aerial photo of Sairyō Kofun]]
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped burial mound]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Misaki, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
[[file:080504 Sairyou Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|right|Aerial photo of Sairyō Kofun]]
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Misaki, Osaka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
[[file:080504 Sairyou Kofun aerial.jpg|thumb|right|Aerial photo of Sairyō Kofun]]
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018年5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{Ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[Sue ware]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[Tannowa Station]] on the [[Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>
