<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sairyo Kofun]][[en:Sairyō Kofun]][[ja:西陵古墳]][[zh:西陵古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629965]] Wikidata name: Sairyo Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.327031|N|135.169011|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki, Osaka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[:en:Osaka Bay|Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[:en:Silla|Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sairyo Kofun]][[en:Sairyō Kofun]][[ja:西陵古墳]][[zh:西陵古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629965]] Wikidata name: Sairyo Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.327031|N|135.169011|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki, Osaka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[:en:Osaka Bay|Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[:en:Silla|Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sairyo Kofun]][[en:Sairyō Kofun]][[ja:西陵古墳]][[zh:西陵古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629965]] Wikidata name: Sairyo Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.327031|N|135.169011|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki, Osaka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[:en:Osaka Bay|Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[:en:Silla|Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sairyo Kofun]][[en:Sairyō Kofun]][[ja:西陵古墳]][[zh:西陵古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629965]] Wikidata name: Sairyo Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.327031|N|135.169011|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki, Osaka]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[:en:Osaka Bay|Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[:en:Silla|Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>

{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[:en:Osaka Bay|Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[:en:Silla|Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>

To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers

{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[:en:Osaka Bay|Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[:en:Silla|Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers

The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[:en:Silla|Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers

{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|keyhole-shaped Kofun]], located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Misaki, Osaka|Misaki, Osaka]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''[[:en:Zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking [[:en:Osaka Bay|Osaka Bay]], and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in [[:en:Wakayama (city)|Wakayama]], so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer [[:en:Silla|Silla]], but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from [[:en:Tannowa Station|Tannowa Station]] on the [[:en:Nankai Main Line|Nankai Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|WD=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay}}, and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama}}, so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Tannowa Station|en|Tannowa Station|WD=Q7683705|lt=Tannowa Station}} on the {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Osaka)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sairyo Kofun]][[en:Sairyō Kofun]][[ja:西陵古墳]][[zh:西陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sairyo Kofun]][[en:Sairyō Kofun]][[ja:西陵古墳]][[zh:西陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sairyo Kofun]][[en:Sairyō Kofun]][[ja:西陵古墳]][[zh:西陵古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11629965]] Wikidata name: Sairyo Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:01, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.327031|N|135.169011|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun}}, located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|WD=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay}}, and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama}}, so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Tannowa Station|en|Tannowa Station|WD=Q7683705|lt=Tannowa Station}} on the {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>

It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|WD=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay}}, and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama}}, so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Tannowa Station|en|Tannowa Station|WD=Q7683705|lt=Tannowa Station}} on the {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>

The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sairyo Kofun]][[en:Sairyō Kofun]][[ja:西陵古墳]][[zh:西陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Sairyo Kofun]][[en:Sairyō Kofun]][[ja:西陵古墳]][[zh:西陵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sairyo Kofun]][[en:Sairyō Kofun]][[ja:西陵古墳]][[zh:西陵古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name =Sairyō Kofun | native_name =西陵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Sairyo Kofun zenkei-1.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Sairyō Kofun, anterior portion to the right, posterior portion to the left | map_type = Japan Osaka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Sairyō Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.327031|N|135.169011|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki, Osaka|1=Misaki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 420-440 AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|19|37.31|N|135|10|8.44|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Sairyō Kofun'''|西陵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Tannowa neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Misaki, Osaka|en|Misaki, Osaka|WD=Q1358900|lt=Misaki, Osaka|1=Misaki, Osaka}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus and its surroundings were designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1922. It is the 28th largest ''kofun'' in Japan<ref name="堺市">[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html 古墳大きさランキング（日本全国版）]（堺市ホームページ、2018th year 5月13日更新版）。</ref> and dates from the 5th century AD.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/1771|title=西陵古墳, 第一、第二古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is also called the {{nihongo|'''Nishi Nisanzai Kofun'''|西二山在古墳|}}
==Overview== The Sairyō Kofun is a {{nihongo|''{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on the riverbank hills of the Ban River overlooking {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|WD=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay|1=Osaka Bay}}, and is orientated to the northeast. The tumulus has a total length of 210 meters with a 115-meter diameter posterior circular portion. The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama|1=Wakayama (city)}}, so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Tannowa Station|en|Tannowa Station|WD=Q7683705|lt=Tannowa Station|1=Tannowa Station}} on the {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line|1=Nankai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
==Gallery== <gallery> File:Sairyo Kofun zenkei-3.JPG|Sairyō Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-1.JPG|Dai-ichi Kofun File:Sairyo Kofun baizuka-2.JPG|Dai-ni Kofun </gallery>

The mound was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]'' and has a square protrusion on the west side of the constriction between the posterior and anterior portions, possibly a ceremonial or offering platform. The tumulus was originally surrounded by a moat with a width of 15 to 35 meters, portions of which survive, but the original shape of the moat is unclear. It is also known that there were three [[:en:Shell midden|Shell midden]]s outside the moat, of which one has been totally destroyed. The Sairyō Kofun is part of a larger cluster which includes the Nishikoyama Kofun, the 170-meter Tannowa Nisanzai Kofun and a number of smaller tumuli.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Details of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] are not clear. In the past a {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} cover stone with two protrusions for rope-hanging on its long side was exposed at the apex of the posterior circular portion, but this has been backfilled after the designation as a national historic site. It is presumed that the burial chamber itself was a pit-type stone chamber made of blocks of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The age of this ''kofun'' is estimated to be around 420 to 440 AD from the style of excavated ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', which are also of a unique pattern found only at this site and at the Kinomoto Kofun Cluster in {{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|WD=Q200747|lt=Wakayama|1=Wakayama (city)}}, so it is assumed that the same ancient clan that built this tumulus controlled a territory which extended into the [[Kii Peninsula]].<ref name="Shiseki"/> According to some historians, the tumulus may be the grave of General Kikoyumi, whose name appears in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' under the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yuryaku|Emperor Yuryaku]]. According to the ''Nihon Shoki'', this general was sent to conquer {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, but died in Korea due to illness and his body was returned to be buried at a place called "Tamuwa no Mura", which is believed to have been an ancient name for Tannowa.
To the north of the Sairyō Kofun are two small {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=circular-type|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ({{nihongo|''empun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}), designated the "Dai-ichi Kofun" and the "Dai-ni Kofun". These tumuli are also within the National Historic Site designation. There was once a third circular-type tumulus, but this was destroyed in modern times when a road was constructed, At that time, a number of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} earthenware jars and fragments of iron swords were found.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a 15-minute walk from {{ill|Tannowa Station|en|Tannowa Station|WD=Q7683705|lt=Tannowa Station|1=Tannowa Station}} on the {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|WD=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line|1=Nankai Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Total length: 96 meters: ;Anterior rectangular portion: 100 meters long x 14 meters high, 3-tier ;West Constriction: 23 x 13 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 115 meter diameter x 18 meters high, 3-tiers
